title: chaetochloa geniculata var. perennis
intro: Chaetochloa geniculata var. perennis, also known as perennial finger grass, is a type of grass native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve flatulence, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's health benefits include its antispasmodic and expectorant properties, which can help to calm the nervous system and promote the removal of mucus from the lungs. Chaetochloa geniculata var. perennis is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for its medicinal properties.
